title: dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica
intro: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica, commonly known as the Atlantic marsh-orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in moist to wet habitats, such as marshes and meadows. The Atlantic marsh-orchid is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which exhibit therapeutic actions such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
benefits: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has many benefits, such as possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, thereby improving overall health and well-being. This plant has also been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections, while its antioxidant properties help protect against cell damage and promote healthy aging. Additionally, Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, and its antiseptic properties make it an effective remedy for oral health issues, including gum disease and bad breath. By harnessing the benefits of this plant, individuals can experience improved health, increased energy, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to live more comfortably and confidently.
actions: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, improving the quality of life for individuals who struggle with these issues. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help relieve muscle cramps, spasms, and menstrual cramps, allowing people to engage in physical activities and daily tasks without discomfort. The plant's diuretic properties can also aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections and edema, promoting a sense of well-being and comfort. Furthermore, its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and infection, supporting overall health and increasing the quality of life for individuals who use it as a natural remedy.
constituents: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has many active constituents, such as iridoids, glycosides, saponins, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include compounds like 7-de-O-β-glucopyranosyl-3,4-dihydro-5α-androstane, 7-de-O-β-glucopyranosyl-5α-androstane, and dactyliol, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, improve heart health, and prevent infections. The iridoids in Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica, such as 7-de-O-β-glucopyranosyl-3,4-dihydro-5α-androstane and dactyliol, also have been found to possess wound-healing properties, promoting faster recovery from injuries and surgical procedures. By harnessing these medicinal constituents, Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica offers potential benefits for individuals seeking natural remedies to enhance their overall well-being and quality of life.
parts: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The roots of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica contain iridoid glycosides, including elatioside and elatiosidin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica contain flavonoid glycosides, including kaempferol-3-O-rutinoside, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea made from this plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The tincture of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica is used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations, as it has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's infusions are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its soothing and calming effects.
side_effects: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to cardiovascular problems, including low blood pressure and irregular heartbeat. In rare cases, excessive use of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. atlantica can also trigger respiratory issues, such as bronchospasm and shortness of breath. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
